mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and financial stress, so when policy is already near the zero lower bound and stays unchanged or declines, equity risk premia compress and the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods with flat-to-lower FEDFUNDS do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered periods, or if the sign is negative in more than 55% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals deteriorating household income and weaker earnings expectations, which depresses equity prices over the next month and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-shock episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average 20-day return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and INDPRO both accelerate together, it signals synchronized demand and production improvement that lifts revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint acceleration in RSAFS and INDPRO does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in the majority of joint-acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST relative to its recent level signals a housing-demand slowdown that precedes softer consumption and risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if large housing starts drops are not followed by weaker 20-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the sign is positive in most post-drop windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation running above target while FEDFUNDS remains exceptionally low implies easier real financial conditions and a supportive liquidity backdrop, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of elevated CPI and very low FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once each series is considered separately.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO indicates rising industrial output and stronger near-term earnings capacity, so a sustained upward trend in industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month INDPRO uptrends do not precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the average forward return is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: If GDP growth is weak while retail sales remain strong, the market reads the economy as resilient but not overheating, supporting earnings confidence and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-RSAFS combination does not improve predictive power versus either proxy alone, or if forward returns are not positive when retail sales strength offsets weak GDP.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight-labor regime, a rising PAYEMS series with stable UNRATE confirms broad job creation without slack, which supports cyclical demand and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll gains with stable unemployment do not lead to higher 60-day SPX returns than baseline, or if the effect reverses when UNRATE is not low.
